titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the John Willis Photographs
titlestmt.author: by Jessica Tai

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday August 16, 2022 at 03:03

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection documents Willis' career as a photographer from the 1970s through 2019. It contains approximately 20,000 film negatives and corresponding contact prints. Photographs by Willis include black and white 35mm negatives, medium format 6x6cm, 6x7cm, 6x17cm negatives, and large format 4x5", 5x7" and 8x10" negatives and contact prints for all images published in Recycled Realities and Views From the Reservation , as well as negatives and contact prints for exhibition prints in Beinecke's collections. The collection includes digital files from Willis' series Mni Wiconi/ Water Is Life: Honoring the Water Protectors at Standing Rock and Everywhere in the Ongoing Struggle for Indigenous Sovereignty that were made during his visits to the site of the Dakota Access Pipeline protests. The collection also contains Willis' darkroom notebook containing technical information on his printing process. Other material includes book mock ups, CD-ROMs, correspondence, and newsletters.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 40-41 (born digital): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Electronic images are available only in Reading Room and not in the Beinecke Digital Library.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The John Willis Photographs is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from John Willis on the Walter McClintock Memorial Fund and the Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana, 2017-2021.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: John Willis Photographs. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection or revisions in arrangement and description.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This collection is organized into three groupings: I. September 2017 Acquisition, II. September 2018 Acquisition, III. June 2021 Acquisition.

bioghist.head: John Willis
bioghist.p: John Willis is a documentary photographer and emeritus professor of photography at Marlboro College in Marlboro, Vermont. His monographs include Recycled Realities , co-authored with Tom Young, Views from the Reservation , and Mni Wiconi/ Water Is Life: Honoring the Water Protectors at Standing Rock and Everywhere in the Ongoing Struggle for Indigenous Sovereignty . Willis has photographs in the permanent collections of the Whitney Museum of American Art, the Museum of Fine Arts in Boston, and the Museum of Fine Arts in Houston, among others. He is also a co-founder with Bill Ledger of the In-Sight Photography Project, a youth photography outreach project in Brattleboro, Vermont.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection documents Willis' career as a photographer from the 1970s through 2019. It contains approximately 20,000 film negatives and corresponding contact prints. Photographs by Willis include black and white 35mm negatives, medium format 6x6cm, 6x7cm, 6x17cm negatives, and large format 4x5", 5x7" and 8x10" negatives and contact prints for all images published in Recycled Realities and Views From the Reservation. as well as negatives and contact prints for exhibition prints in Beinecke's collections. The collection contains platinum prints for the series, Aging in White in addition to a series of Polaroids taken by Linden Lodge nursing home residents participating in Willis' instant photography course in 1985.

The collection includes digital files from Willis' series Mni Wiconi/ Water Is Life: Honoring the Water Protectors at Standing Rock and Everywhere in the Ongoing Struggle for Indigenous Sovereignty that were made during his visits to the site of the Dakota Access Pipeline protests. The collection also contains Willis' darkroom notebook containing technical information on his printing process. Other material includes book mock ups, CD-ROMs, correspondence, and newsletters.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: John Willis Collection of Lakota Art (WA MSS S-4447). Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

John Willis Photographs of Arizona, Nebraska, New Mexico, North Dakota, Pine Ridge Indian Reservation, and South Dakota (WA Photos Folio 148). Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.
John Willis Photographs of Arizona, Cambodia, New Mexico, and the Pine Ridge Indian Reservation (WA Photos Folio 149). Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.
John Willis Photographs of Arizona, Connecticut, New Mexico, and South Dakota (WA Photos Folio 155). Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.
John Willis Photographs of Arizona, Nebraska, New Mexico, Pine Ridge Indian Reservation, and South Dakota (WA Photos Folio 185). Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.
John Willis Photographs of Dakota Access Pipeline Protests in North Dakota (WA Photos Folio 186). Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.
John Willis Photographs of Nebraska, North Dakota, South Dakota and the Mexican-American Border Region (WA Photos Folio 213). Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.
John Willis Photographs of Pine Ridge Indian Reservation and South Dakota (WA Photos Folio 219). Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

geogname.part: Standing Rock Indian Reservation (N.D. and S.D.)
Standing Rock Sioux Tribe of North & South Dakota

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from John Willis on the Walter McClintock Memorial Fund, 2017.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of computer files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains CDs and DVDs, digital files, and one hard drive. CDs and DVDs were created by Willis during the creation of the project, Views From the Reservation. Digital files include copies of his work, Lakota artwork and music, drafts and master files for the CD, "Heartbeat of the Rez," Reddest family archive images, images from the newspaper Akwasasne Notes , collage document files and collages Willis made for the book's endpapers. Additional CDs document the creation of an eight-panel panorama from 4"x5" type 55 negatives that Willis made for the Wounded Knee Mass Gravesite as well as aerial photographs of a Sundance site in Thunder Valley. The hard drive contains digital image files documenting protests against the Dakota Access Pipeline project at Standing Rock Reservation.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from John Willis on the Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana, 2018.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The acquisition contains approximately 20,000 film negatives and corresponding contact prints. Photographs by Willis include black and white 35mm negatives, medium format 6x6cm, 6x7cm, 6x17cm negatives, and large format 4x5", 5x7" and 8x10" negatives and contact prints. Other material includes book mock ups, correspondence, and newsletters.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into the following series: I. Photographic work, 1978-2007, II. Mni Wiconi, Honoring the Water Protectors, 2016-2019, III. Recycled Realities, 1998-2001, IV. Views from the Reservation, 1990-2019, V. Professional material, 1880-2010.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into three subseries: Negatives, 1978-2007; Contact Sheets, 1983-1997; Prints, 1997. Material was originally received in binders or boxes organized by date. These groupings have been maintained.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 35mm, medium format 6x6cm, 6x7cm, 6x17cm, and large format 4"x5", 5"x7", and 8"x10" negatives. Contact sheets that were originally integrated with negatives have been left in place. Labelled binders originally housing negatives have been documented in the folder title.

scopecontent.p: Includes negatives labelled "Jeff the Bon."

Black and white 11" x 14" silver gelatin prints

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs featuring images of trees, a Civilian Conservation Corps sign, and a forest landscape.

did.unittitle: Mni Wiconi, Honoring the Water Protectors

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes a book proof, book drafts, photographs, and project files for Mni Wiconi - Water Is Life Honoring the Water Protectors at Standing Rock and Everywhere in the Ongoing Struggle for Indigenous Sovereignty (George F. Thompson Publishing, 2019). Willis created the body of work from September 2016 to December 2017 over the course of six trips to witness the efforts of the Dakota Access Pipeline protests. Willis visited the Oceti Sakowin ("Seven Council Fires") Camp in North Dakota, one of several camps set up to accommodate the resisters and their allies.

did.unittitle: Book draft: "Mni Wiconi: Honoring the Water Protectors, George F. Thompson copy" book draft
Project files: clippings, interviews, photographs, poems, and timelines
Project files: interviews, notes, photographs, and poems
Project files: notes and book proof

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains prints and contact sheets from Willis' photographic series, Recycled Realities . Willis created the images between the years of 1998 and 2001 at a paper mill in the hills of Western Massachusetts in collaboration with New England-based photographer Tom Young.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes 35mm, medium and large format black and white negatives and contact sheets from Views from the Reservation (George F Thompson Publishing, 2019). The images from this body of work were created during Willis' visits to the Pine Ridge Reservation, tribal home of the Oglala Lakota Sioux People. Since 1992, Willis traveled there regularly to visit the Reddest family of Lost Dog Creek. Willis acknowledges his positionality as a non-Indigenous photographer documenting the lives of Indigenous people. In his artist statement, Willis states, "As an outsider it is always an honor to be welcomed into their home." This series also includes a book proof, correspondence, prints, and the final publication for the project.

did.unittitle: 35mm, medium and large black and white format negatives: "Views from the Reservation book negatives"
35mm, medium, and large format black and white negatives
35mm and medium format black and white negatives and contact sheet: "Timsila wild turnip pine ridge," "Snow scene Ames Hill Road"
35mm and medium format black and white negatives: "Views from the Reservation book negatives"
35mm, medium, and large format black and white negatives: "Lost Dog Creek"
35mm, medium, and large format black and white negatives: "Lost Dog negatives"
Medium and large format black and white negatives: "Pine Ridge Exposures trip"
Book proof: Views from the Reservation
Correspondence: letter containing book endorsement quote from Ken Burns
Prints: "Views from the Rez RC, 2nds, outtakes"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains material from Willis' professional activities as a photographer, including his darkroom notebook, newsletters and promotional material from the In-Sight Photography Project.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Darkroom notebook maintained while Willis was making final prints throughout the years of creating Recycled Realities and Views From the Reservation . The notebook documents Willis' experiments while attempting to gain control over the split toning techniques used in Recycled Realities and included information on water temperature, water pressure, chemical dilutions, developer formula, and developer temperature.

did.unittitle: The In-Sight Photography Project: newsletters and promotional material

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from John Willis on the Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana, 2021.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series of platinum print photographs from the series "Aging in White." Housed in mat boards.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series of 15 Polaroid photographs housed in mat boards. Taken by Linden Lodge nursing home residents participating in Willis' Polaroid photography course in the summer of 1985. The class was sponsored by the Polaroid Foundation and the Brattleboro Retreat Medical Facility where Linden Lodge is located.
